# Deployment Diagram — RAG Refinement System (MVP topology)

```mermaid
graph TB
    subgraph clients["Clients"]
        SPA["Browser SPA<br/>React + Vite (ADR-9)"]
        ENTRAG["Enterprise RAG client"]
    end

    subgraph cloud["Cloud (Docker, 12-factor)"]
        LB["Load Balancer / Ingress"]
        subgraph apinodes["API tier (HLD §10: 2 replicas)"]
            API1["FastAPI replica 1<br/>/health /ready (NFR-009)"]
            API2["FastAPI replica 2<br/>/health /ready"]
        end
        QDNODE[("Qdrant node x1<br/>chunk vectors (ADR-2)")]
        PGNODE[("PostgreSQL x1<br/>documents, sections (ADR-10)")]
        OBJNODE[("Object Storage<br/>raw uploads")]
    end

    subgraph providers["External Providers (HLD §2)"]
        ANTH["Anthropic API"]
        OAI["OpenAI API"]
        COH["Cohere API"]
        AZ["Azure Doc Intelligence"]
        LS["LangSmith"]
    end

    SPA -->|HTTPS / SSE| LB
    ENTRAG -->|HTTPS| LB
    LB --> API1
    LB --> API2
    API1 --> QDNODE
    API1 --> PGNODE
    API1 --> OBJNODE
    API2 --> QDNODE
    API2 --> PGNODE
    API2 --> OBJNODE
    API1 --> ANTH
    API1 --> OAI
    API1 --> COH
    API1 --> AZ
    API1 -.traces.-> LS
```

> MVP sizing (2 API replicas + 1 Qdrant + 1 Postgres) is the HLD §10 Little's-Law result. India-residency tenants map to region-pinned Qdrant/Postgres/object-store instances (OAQ-5).
